page: Microsoft Azure E8-2as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Easv7 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with multithreading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per vCPU. The CPU includes a 32 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and AES cryptography. Local storage is not bundled, but the instance is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals a significant performance asymmetry. Single-core performance is highly competitive, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all ranking in the top tier. However, due to the low core count of 2 vCPUs, multi-core performance is limited, with stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores falling into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is average, while cached small-block read bandwidth is relatively weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a high density of memory relative to its compute footprint, making it a viable option for workloads that require substantial memory capacity but do not demand intensive multi-core processing. It is well-suited for memory-intensive, single-threaded or low-concurrency workloads, such as small-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, lightweight data processing, and applications requiring high single-core execution speeds paired with a large memory footprint.
description: Microsoft Azure E8-2as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Easv7 family, featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz, this x86_64 instance delivers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core. While it lacks local storage, it is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark results highlight a distinct performance profile: single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression rank in the top 10%, whereas multi-core CPU performance and database operations fall into the bottom 25% due to the low vCPU count. This server is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-heavy applications with low parallel processing requirements. It is best suited for memory-intensive workloads, caching layers, and single-threaded data processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8-2as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, ideal for memory-heavy, low-concurrency workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine combining high single-core clock speeds with an exceptional memory-to-vCPU ratio for memory-intensive workloads.

Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, offering 32.0 GB of memory per vCPU
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage
Best suited for memory-intensive, low-concurrency applications and caching layers
